reasoning:
The resolution evaluates whether FANG's March 20 closing price is higher than its March 13 closing price. Three key factors strongly suggest it will be higher:
1. Under in-game constraints, FANG closed at $179 on March 6. On March 11, heavily discounted secondary offering of 11M shares priced at ~$172.72. This likely depressed the stock on Thursday/Friday (Mar 12-13).
2. The U.S. launched military strikes against Iran's Kharg Island on Friday, March 13, and over the weekend, leading WTI crude to gap up over $100 by Sunday, March 15. The Friday March 13 closing price did not fully price in the Sunday oil surge.
3. Because the stock market will gap up for oil equities on Monday, March 16, FANG will begin the week significantly higher than its March 13 close. While a peace deal is theoretically possible (Trump's "Tuesday peace deal deadline"), the presence of sea mines in the Strait of Hormuz and ongoing supply disruptions make a full unwinding of the oil spike by March 20 highly improbable. The depressed base of March 13 plus the massive structural tailwinds for oil strongly points to a YES.
